name: Midland States Bank
description_short: Midland States Bank offers personal and business banking with checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and wealth management services. Member FDIC with multiple account types and financial planning tools.
description_long:
Midland States Bank is a full-service financial institution providing comprehensive banking services to personal, small business, and commercial customers. The bank operates a network of physical locations and offers digital banking platforms for customer convenience. Based on their website, they position themselves as a community-focused institution that emphasizes personalized service and customer relationships. Midland States Bank has been operating as an established financial institution with multiple service lines and a established customer base.

The bank offers a diverse range of financial products including personal checking and savings accounts at multiple tier levels (Totally Free Checking, Direct Interest Checking, High Yield Checking), personal loans, mortgages, credit cards, and business banking solutions for small to mid-sized enterprises. Their wealth management division includes trust and investment services, with partnerships including Raymond James and Epic Advisor platforms for financial advisory. They also provide financial calculators, solution finders, and educational tutorials to help customers understand and manage their finances.

Midland States Bank differentiates itself through a referral program offering up to $500 in rewards, emphasis on personalized customer service (staff greeting customers by name), and multi-channel support including phone, email, chat, and in-person appointments. They market themselves as community-oriented with frequent market commentary and tax planning resources. The bank emphasizes simplicity in their checking products and positioning around "moments" and life events. Multiple login portals indicate sophisticated digital infrastructure supporting personal, business, mortgage, credit card, and wealth management services separately.

As a traditional bank, Midland States Bank serves customers seeking FDIC-insured deposit products and comprehensive financial services. However, the website provides limited specific information about interest rates, loan terms, fees (beyond mentioning no monthly service charge on some accounts), credit requirements, or competitive positioning. The lack of detailed product specifications, APR ranges, or fee schedules limits ability to fully assess value proposition relative to competitors. Their wealth management services appear robust but are presented vaguely on the main site. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Totally Free Checking accounts with no minimum balance or monthly fees
Direct Interest Checking with interest earnings and automatic payment features
High Yield Checking for customers seeking higher interest returns
Mortgage lending including home purchase and refinance
Credit cards for personal and business use
Investment advisory through Raymond James partnership
Financial planning through Epic Advisor platform
Online and mobile banking platforms with separate portals for personal, business, and wealth services
pros: Multiple checking account tiers with no minimum balance on Totally Free Checking and no monthly service charges
Up to $100 bonus for opening new checking and savings accounts with qualifying direct deposit
Referral program offering up to $500 in rewards for successful friend referrals
Comprehensive wealth management services including trust, investments, Raymond James, and Epic Advisor partnerships
FDIC member bank providing deposit insurance protection
Multiple contact methods: phone (1-855-696-4352), email, chat, appointment booking, and branch locations
Free financial planning tools including calculators, solution finder, and step-by-step tutorials
cons: Website provides no specific information about interest rates, APYs, or loan APRs for any products
Limited details on fees beyond checking account statements (mortgage origination fees, business account fees, etc. not disclosed)
No clear information about credit requirements, loan approval process, or eligibility criteria
Wealth management services appear to require separate logins and may have minimum balance requirements not disclosed on main site
Marketing-heavy homepage with limited product specification detail, requiring customers to contact directly for pricing
best_for: Customers seeking traditional FDIC-insured checking and savings accounts with personalized local service
Small business owners needing comprehensive banking, lending, and cash management solutions
Individuals interested in integrated wealth management, trust services, and investment advisory alongside banking
diagnosis: Midland States Bank is best for customers seeking a full-service traditional bank with local branch presence, FDIC-insured deposits, and integrated wealth management. The main caveat is that the website lacks transparent pricing information on interest rates, APYs, and fees—customers must contact the bank directly or visit branches to compare offerings against competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
